title: moraea alticola
intro: Moraea alticola, also known as the mountain marsh marigold, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's roots and leaves are used to prepare various herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and promoting digestive health. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Moraea alticola as a medicinal plant, and its use should be approached with caution.
